label: 1453 CE
era_summary: Fall of Constantinople to Mehmed II. Hundred Years' War ends. Ming dynasty mid-stride. Italian Renaissance flowering. Black Death's worst century, but past peak.

rome_italy.summary: Renaissance city-states (Florence, Venice, Milan) — wealthy, urban, intellectually vibrant. Plague recurrences but past 1348 peak.

han_china.summary: Ming dynasty under Jingtai emperor. Post-Zheng He retrenchment, but centralized, prosperous, food-secure.

gupta_india.summary: Delhi Sultanate post-Timur (1398) weak; regional sultanates (Bahmani, Vijayanagara) competing.

persia.summary: Post-Timurid fragmentation. Black Sheep / White Sheep Turkomans warring.

egypt_nile.summary: Mamluk Sultanate — stable rule, but plague-stricken Cairo, declining trade as Portuguese threaten Indian Ocean routes.

constantinople_balkans.summary: Constantinople falls May 29, 1453. Mass casualties, enslavement. Avoid.

andalusia_iberia.summary: Castile + Aragon recovering, Reconquista active (Granada still Muslim). Plague waves but trade booming.

francia.summary: End of Hundred Years' War (1453) — English expelled. Devastated countryside, recovery beginning.

england.summary: Post-war, Henry VI's reign collapsing. Wars of the Roses imminent (1455).

scandinavia.summary: Kalmar Union under Christian I — formally united Denmark/Norway/Sweden. Cold, but stable.

rus.summary: Muscovy rising under Vasily II, tribute to Golden Horde weakening. Stable but cold.

central_asia.summary: Timurid renaissance under Ulugh Beg's recent legacy — Samarkand, Herat are cultural centers.

japan.summary: Late Muromachi shogunate weak. Onin War (1467) approaching. Sengoku unrest brewing.

mesoamerica.summary: Aztec Triple Alliance under Moctezuma I — expanding, well-fed (chinampas), stable for nobles. Brutal for tributaries.

andes.summary: Inca expansion under Pachacuti — terraced agriculture, road network, stable empire emerging.

sahel_west_africa.summary: Songhai Empire rising (Sunni Ali soon to take throne 1464). Mali declining but Timbuktu still cultural center.

ethiopia.summary: Solomonic dynasty under Zara Yaqob (d. 1468) — strong centralization, Christian orthodox stability.

north_america_east.summary: Mississippian culture mature (Cahokia past peak but regional chiefdoms strong). Iroquois confederacy forming.
